habitat: Tropical/subtropical evergreen broadleaf forest edges; open woodlands; and cultivated areas
story: Explore the fascinating world of the Oriented Pied Hornbill (Anthracoceros albirostris). Classified under the Bucerotidae family, this bird predominantly inhabits Tropical/subtropical evergreen broadleaf forest edges; open woodlands; and cultivated areas. The current IUCN classification of Least Concern (LC) underscores the importance of safeguarding its natural range. From its feeding habits to its nesting preferences, the Oriented Pied Hornbill is a testament to the intricate web of life. By studying species like the Oriented Pied Hornbill, we gain valuable insights into broader ecological trends.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
